claim: Hand A's data satisfies three external structural predictions made independently by prior researchers in the Voynich literature: (a) Stolfi's crust-only fraction ~75%; (b) Stolfi's prefix/suffix dealer asymmetry ~0.14 vs ~0.80; (c) Caveney's word-final n frequency >=85%. If Brain-V's Hand A characterisation is correct, all three should pass. The number of passing predictions out of three quantifies external convergence between Brain-V's autonomous findings and 25 years of prior Voynich structural analysis.
evidence_for: Stolfi's crust-mantle-core grammar (2000, voynich.nu/hist/stolfi/grammar.html) is the best-established Voynich word-structure model; its crust-only fraction (~75%) and prefix/suffix asymmetry (0.14 vs 0.80) are derived primarily from Currier B but stated as applying to the whole manuscript.

Caveney (2020, voynich.ninja thread 3354) made a specific, testable positional claim — word-final n >=90% — as part of a Basque-leaning proposal. This is the only pre-Brain-V Basque-language-adjacent proposal with a quantitative structural prediction concrete enough to pre-register against.

Brain-V's Basque-leaning Hand A findings emerged independently, without reference to Stolfi or Caveney. If the findings are structurally valid, external convergence is expected; if not, at least one of the three tests should fail.
All three predictions pre-date Brain-V (Stolfi by ~25 years, Caveney by ~5 years). They are therefore unbiased external benchmarks — pass/fail cannot be influenced by Brain-V's internal framing.
evidence_against: Stolfi derived his numbers primarily from Currier B; Hand A may have systematically different crust/mantle/core ratios even if the layer paradigm holds. The 75% crust-only figure may be a B-specific statistic mis-applied to A.

Caveney's word-final n figure (>=90%) was stated as an impression on a forum, not derived from a rigorous corpus measurement. The prediction itself may be imprecise. Pass band was loosened to [0.85, 1.00] accordingly.

Stolfi's prefix/suffix dealer counts (0.14 vs 0.80) require an operational definition of 'productive stem' and 'dealer'. Brain-V's definition may not match Stolfi's exactly, introducing measurement bias that could cause a pass or fail for reasons other than the underlying claim.

Convergence does not imply correctness: three prior researchers could all be reflecting the same systematic artefact in the EVA transliteration rather than a genuine linguistic structure. A pass on all three is compatible with Brain-V and the prior researchers all being wrong in the same direction.
test:
LOCKED PROCEDURE.

  DATA: Hand A corpus, EVA transliteration. Use the same folio partition as H-BV-BASQUE-* series. Hand A tokens only; Hand B excluded from all three tests.

  TEST 1 — STOLFI CRUST-ONLY FRACTION:
    Stolfi layer categories (LOCKED per Stolfi 2000, voynich.nu/hist/stolfi/grammar.html):
      CORE:   {t, p, k, f, cth, cph, ckh, cfh}
      MANTLE: {ch, sh, ee}
      CRUST:  {d, l, r, s, n, x, i, m, g, q, o, y, a, e}
    Tokenise each Hand A word into Stolfi units. A token is 'crust-only' if it contains zero core glyphs and zero mantle ligatures. Compute:
      crust_only_fraction = |{tokens with no core and no mantle}| / |all Hand A tokens|
    PASS if crust_only_fraction in [0.70, 0.80].
    FAIL otherwise.

  TEST 2 — STOLFI PREFIX/SUFFIX DEALER ASYMMETRY:
    For each Hand A token with at least one core glyph, split the token at the first core glyph into (prefix, core, suffix). Stolfi's 'dealer' count per position = mean number of crust glyphs in that position across all split tokens.
    Compute:
      prefix_dealer_mean = mean number of crust glyphs in prefix across all split tokens
      suffix_dealer_mean = mean number of crust glyphs in suffix across all split tokens
      asymmetry_ratio    = prefix_dealer_mean / suffix_dealer_mean
    Stolfi's published values (without q): prefix ~0.14, suffix ~0.80, ratio ~0.175.
    PASS if asymmetry_ratio < 0.25.
    FAIL if asymmetry_ratio >= 0.25.
    NOTE: exclude q-initial tokens from this test per Stolfi's original specification.

  TEST 3 — CAVENEY WORD-FINAL n FREQUENCY:
    For each Hand A token, check whether the final glyph (after EVA tokenisation) is 'n'.
    Compute:
      final_n_fraction = |{Hand A tokens ending in 'n'}| / |all Hand A tokens|
    PASS if final_n_fraction in [0.85, 1.00].
    FAIL otherwise.
    NOTE: Caveney's original claim was 'word-final or coda position >=90%'. Because 'coda' is ambiguous in EVA, the locked test uses strict word-final only. Pass band loosened to 0.85 to absorb the coda/final-only distinction.

  DECISION RULE (LOCKED):
    STRONG CONVERGENCE: 3/3 predictions pass. Brain-V's Hand A characterisation aligns with three independent prior frameworks. The composite-outer hypothesis (test (3) of the v2 plan) becomes a refinement of an already-validated structural picture.
    MODERATE CONVERGENCE: 2/3 pass. Identify which specific prediction fails. Document what the failure implies about Brain-V's framework. Composite-outer hypothesis still worth running but with explicit acknowledgment of the divergence.
    WEAK/NO CONVERGENCE: 0-1/3 pass. External predictions disagree with Brain-V's data. Either the prior researchers were wrong, or Brain-V has a systematic measurement error. v2 must address this explicitly — either by identifying a defensible reason the priors don't apply to Hand A, or by re-examining Brain-V's tokenisation/statistical pipeline.

  REPORTING:
    For each test, record: the raw statistic, the pass band, the pass/fail verdict. Do not retrospectively adjust pass bands — they are locked by this file.
test_threshold: STRONG if 3/3. MODERATE if 2/3. WEAK if 0-1/3.
tests_remaining: locked_external_predictions_test: run Tests 1, 2, 3 on Hand A corpus with locked pass bands; report 3-vector of pass/fail plus raw statistics
reasoning: Prior-work literature search (2026-04-17, saved to outputs/reports/prior-work-basque-voynich.md) established that Brain-V's Basque-leaning Hand A findings emerged independently of the published Voynich structural literature. Three external predictions are concrete enough to pre-register against: two from Stolfi (2000), one from Caveney (2020). Havrankova (2025) was considered but contains zero quantitative predictions, so cannot be locked against. Testing Brain-V's Hand A data against these three external predictions provides an unbiased convergence check before designing the composite-outer hypothesis test (v2 test 3). A strong external convergence result would let the composite-outer test be pre-registered as a refinement of an already-validated picture; a weak convergence would force a re-examination of Brain-V's tokenisation pipeline before further Basque-specific testing.
source: Brain-V 2026-04-17 pre-registration derived from prior-work search findings (outputs/reports/prior-work-basque-voynich.md). External predictions sourced from: Stolfi 2000 (voynich.nu/hist/stolfi/grammar.html) and Caveney 2020 (voynich.ninja thread 3354).
